code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta

    # Parameters
    atr_period = params['atr_period']
    multiplier_short = params['multiplier_short']
    multiplier_long = params['multiplier_long']
    adx_threshold = params['adx_threshold']

    # Supertrend indicators
    st_short = ta.trend.Supertrend(high=df['high'], low=df['low'], close=df['close'], window=atr_period, multiplier=multiplier_short)
    st_long = ta.trend.Supertrend(high=df['high'], low=df['low'], close=df['close'], window=atr_period, multiplier=multiplier_long)

    # Supertrend directions: 1 = uptrend, -1 = downtrend
    st_short_direction = st_short.supertrend_signal()  # 1 for up, -1 for down
    st_long_direction = st_long.supertrend_signal()

    # ADX filter
    adx = ta.trend.ADXIndicator(high=df['high'], low=df['low'], close=df['close'], window=atr_period)
    adx_values = adx.adx()

    # Signals: Long when short ST flips up (crosses above long ST) and ADX > threshold
    # Short when short ST flips down (crosses below long ST) and ADX > threshold
    long_condition = (st_short_direction == 1) & (st_long_direction == 1) & (adx_values > adx_threshold)
    short_condition = (st_short_direction == -1) & (st_long_direction == -1) & (adx_values > adx_threshold)

    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    signals[long_condition] = 1
    signals[short_condition] = -1

    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: Gold tends to trend strongly after volatility expansions; dual Supertrend with different multipliers creates a breakout filter where short ST crossing into alignment with long ST confirms trend initiation, and ADX ensures the trend is strong enough to persist.

self_critique.why_this_might_be_noise: Supertrend is a lagging indicator that can whipsaw in ranging markets; ADX threshold may filter out valid breakouts in low-volatility environments.
self_critique.what_would_disprove_this: Consistent losses during gold's known consolidation periods (e.g., when ADX stays below 20 for extended time) would invalidate the trend-following premise.
